meta.description: Candlestick pattern recognition rules for pure price-based signals. Includes detection math, signal meaning, and required context.

corePrinciple: A candlestick pattern is ONLY a signal when it forms AT a key location (support/resistance, order block, fib zone) AND aligns with bias. Patterns in the middle of nowhere are noise.

singleCandle.name: Pin Bar / Hammer
singleCandle.detection: lowerWick >= 2 * body AND upperWick <= 0.3 * body AND forms after a decline
singleCandle.context: At support/demand zone. Entry on break of pin high, SL below pin low.

singleCandle.name: Shooting Star / Inverted Hammer at top
singleCandle.detection: upperWick >= 2 * body AND lowerWick <= 0.3 * body AND forms after a rally
singleCandle.context: At resistance/supply zone. Entry on break of star low, SL above star high.

singleCandle.name: Doji
singleCandle.context: After a strong leg = potential exhaustion. Needs next-candle confirmation.

singleCandle.name: Marubozu
singleCandle.context: Full-bodied candle = momentum. Bullish marubozu breaking resistance = valid breakout.

singleCandle.name: Spinning Top
singleCandle.detection: small body, wicks both sides roughly equal
singleCandle.context: In a trend pullback = pause, likely continuation. At extremes = possible reversal.

doubleCandle.name: Bullish Engulfing
doubleCandle.detection: bearish candle followed by bullish candle whose body fully covers previous body
doubleCandle.context: At demand zone / support with bullish HTF bias = strong entry trigger.

doubleCandle.name: Bearish Engulfing
doubleCandle.detection: bullish candle followed by bearish candle whose body fully covers previous body
doubleCandle.context: At supply zone / resistance with bearish HTF bias = strong entry trigger.

doubleCandle.name: Tweezer Bottom
doubleCandle.detection: two candles with nearly equal lows, second closes bullish

doubleCandle.name: Tweezer Top
doubleCandle.detection: two candles with nearly equal highs, second closes bearish

doubleCandle.name: Inside Bar
doubleCandle.detection: candle fully inside previous candle's range
doubleCandle.context: Trade the break of the mother bar in the trend direction. Fakeout risk: wait for close beyond mother bar.

tripleCandle.name: Morning Star
tripleCandle.detection: big bearish candle -> small indecision candle -> big bullish candle closing above midpoint of first

tripleCandle.name: Evening Star
tripleCandle.detection: big bullish candle -> small indecision candle -> big bearish candle closing below midpoint of first

tripleCandle.name: Three White Soldiers
tripleCandle.detection: three consecutive strong bullish candles with higher closes and small wicks

tripleCandle.name: Three Black Crows
tripleCandle.detection: three consecutive strong bearish candles with lower closes and small wicks

tripleCandle.name: Three Inside Up/Down
tripleCandle.detection: engulfing-like pattern plus a third candle confirming the direction

pureCandleSignalMethod.description: How to get signals purely from candlesticks with no indicators.
steps: 1. Identify the last impulse leg and its direction (count consecutive same-color candles and their sizes).
2. Locate where price is relative to obvious swing highs/lows (raw structure).
3. Wait for price to reach a prior swing area or fresh extreme.
4. Watch wick behaviour: long rejection wicks against the move = absorption.
5. Trigger only on a confirmed pattern CLOSE (never mid-candle).
6. Entry: break of trigger candle high/low. SL: beyond pattern extreme. TP: last opposing swing.
7. Skip any pattern that forms mid-range with no reference level.
wickReading: Long lower wicks repeatedly = buyers defending; look for longs.
Long upper wicks repeatedly = sellers defending; look for shorts.
Shrinking candle bodies in a move = momentum dying.
Expanding bodies in break direction = momentum confirmation.

notes: On Boom indices, bearish candles dominate visually; a single large bullish candle is usually a spike, not a pattern.
On Crash indices, bullish candles dominate; a single large bearish candle is a spike.
Never treat spike candles as engulfing/reversal patterns; exclude spike candles from pattern detection.
Volatility indices (V10-V100) behave like normal markets; all patterns apply.
